DECLARATION OF DAVID AGRETELIS IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFFS MOTION FOR FINAL APPROVAL OF CLASS ACTION SETTLEMENT, CERTIFICATION OF SETTLEMENT CLASS, AWARD OF ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS AND AWARD OF SERVICE PAYMENT TO PLAINTIFFS FILED BY PLAINTIFF THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR FINAL APPROVAL OF CLASS ACTION SETTLEMENT, CERTIFICATION OF SETTLEMENT CLASS, AWARD OF ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS AND AWARD OF SERVICE PAYMENT TO PLAINTIFFS IS GRANTED IN LARGE PART. THE FEES AWARDED TO CLASS COUNSEL ARE REDUCED TO $600,000, WHICH IS 20% OF THE INITIAL SETTLEMENT FUND AND IS A MULTIPLIER IN EXCESS OF 1.4. WHILE CLASS COUNSEL ACHIEVED EXCELLENT RESULTS, THEIR HOURLY RATES ARE QUITE HIGH, CLASS MEMBERS ARE CHARITIES, THE CASE WAS A VERY ATTRACTIVE ONE FROM THE PERSPECTIVE OF A CLASS CONTINGENCY LAWYER, AND A MULTIPLIER OF 1.4 IS SUFFICIENT TO COMPENSATE FOR THE LITIGATION RISKS. MR. MESSICK IS NOT ENTITLED TO A SERVICE AWARD, THE SERVICE AWARD FOR THE REGENTS IS REDUCED TO $5000 AND THE SERVICE AWARD TO BBF IS REDUCED TO $1000. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077.
THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: ON FINAL APPROVAL OF SETTLEMENT (SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT)

calendar.matters:
Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Final Approval Of Class Action Settlement Certification Of Settlement Class Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs And Award Of Service Payment To Plaintiffs
Off Calendar Sep-07-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: michael schrag, esq., meade & schrag, 510.843.3670, for plaintiff the regents of the university of california.
patricial rosenberg, esq., hass najarian llp, 415.788.6330, for defendant hay zalewski.
beth aney, esq., wilson elser, 415.433.0990, for defendant brandon miller.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's Motion For Final Approval Of Class Action Settlement, Certification Of Settlement Class, Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs And Award Of Service Payment To Plaintiffs is Granted in large part. The fees awarded to class counsel are reduced to $600,000, which is 20% of the initial settlement fund and is a multiplier in excess of 1.4. While class counsel achieved excellent results, their hourly rates are quite high, class members are charities, the case was a very attractive one from the perspective of a class contingency lawyer, and a multiplier of 1.4 is sufficient to compensate for the litigation risks. Mr. Messick is not entitled to a service award, the service award for the Regents is reduced to $5000 and the service award to BBF is reduced to $1000. Order signed in open Court. judgment signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.

Plaintiffs' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order (1) Preliminarily Approving Class Action Settlement;(2) Provisionally Certifying Distribution Of Notice Of Settlement To The Class; And (4) Setting Heating Fir Final Approval.

APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL SCHRAG OF MEADE & SCHRAG LLP, TEL. NO. (510) 843-3670 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. BETH A. ANEY OF WILSON ELSER, TEL. NO. 625-9243 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. PATRICIA ROSENBER OF HAAS NAJARIAN LLP, TEL. NO. 788-6330 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF ZALEWSKI;
ATTY. LAURA L. GEIST OF GORDON & REES LLP, TEL. NO. 875-3158 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AMERISE.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. PLAINTIFFS THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, ROBERT MESSICK AND BLIND BABIES FOUNDATION'S Motion For Order (1) Preliminarily Approving Class Action Settlement;(2) Provisionally Certifying Distribution Of Notice Of Settlement To The Class; And (4) Setting hearing For Final Approval is Granted. The settlement appears to be in the range of possible approval at a final hearing. FINAL FAIRNESS HEARING AND FINAL APPROVAL IS CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 7, 2012. The Court will also sign an order regarding the 3 defunct charities. 2 ORDERS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported. = (302/HEK)
